pdg_node.statement: (mat_list: List[np.ndarray], max_cdr3: int, max_epi: int) -> np.ndarray
pdg_node.informal_desc: Takes a list of matrices and pads them to a specified maximum dimension, effectively creating a batched and padded distance matrix representation.

declaration.name: encodedistancematrix
declaration.type_signature: (mat_list: List[np.ndarray], max_cdr3: int, max_epi: int) -> np.ndarray
declaration.docstring: Takes a list of matrices and pads them to a specified maximum dimension, effectively creating a batched and padded distance matrix representation.
declaration.conceptual_summary: StandardizeTensorDimensions. Takes a collection of 2D tensors, each with potentially different dimensions. It pads each tensor with a default value (e.g., zero) up to specified maximum dimensions, creating a set of uniformly-sized tensors. These are then stacked along a new axis to form a single 3D tensor, enabling batched processing.. Applications: Image Processing: Standardizing a batch of images of various sizes into a single tensor for input into a convolutional neural network., Natural Language Processing: Padding batches of sentence embeddings (word vectors) to a uniform length before feeding them into a transformer or recurrent neural network., Spectrogram Analysis: Aligning audio spectrograms of different time durations into a uniform tensor for comparative analysis or model training., Financial Modeling: Batching time-series data of different lengths (e.g., stock price history over different periods) for input into a forecasting model.
